## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On June 23, 1757, the absolute, most massive and historically catastrophic military engagement in modern Indian history was aggressively fought: the Battle of Plassey. The massively outnumbered forces of the British East India Company, heavily commanded by Robert Clive, aggressively defeated the massive army of Siraj-ud-Daulah, the Nawab of Bengal, largely due to the massive, treasonous betrayal by Mir Jafar. This horrific, massive defeat officially and absolutely initiated two centuries of deeply oppressive, massive British colonial rule in India.

On June 23, 1980, Sanjay Gandhi, the incredibly massive, deeply controversial, and highly powerful political heir to Prime Minister Indira Gandhi, tragically died. He was aggressively killed in a massive, fiery plane crash while illegally flying a stunt aircraft over New Delhi. His sudden, massive death created an absolute, deep political void within the Indian National Congress, forcing his highly reluctant older brother, Rajiv Gandhi, to aggressively enter the massive, heavily volatile arena of Indian dynastic politics.
